name: DatePicker

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: An opaque type representing messages that are passed inside the DatePicker.

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: The type of date picker settings.
aliases.type: { placeholder : String, classNamespace : String, inputClassList : List ( String, Bool ), inputName : Maybe String, inputId : Maybe String, isDisabled : Date -> Bool, parser : String -> Result String Date, dateFormatter : Date -> String, dayFormatter : Day -> String, monthFormatter : Month -> String, yearFormatter : Int -> String, cellFormatter : String -> Html Msg, firstDayOfWeek : Day, pickedDate : Maybe Date }

values.name: defaultSettings
getDate
values.comment: Extract the current set date (if set) from a datepicker

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize a DatePicker given a Settings record.  You must execute
the returned command for the date picker to behave correctly.


    init =
      let
         (datePicker, datePickerFx) =
           DatePicker.init defaultSettings
      in
         { picker = datePicker } ! [ Cmd.map ToDatePicker datePickerfx ]

values.name: isOpen
values.comment: Expose if the datepicker is open

values.name: setDate
values.comment: Set a new date in the datepicker

values.name: setFilter
values.comment: Set the function that marks days valid or invalid, so for example if you need to build a date range you can keep those in sync

values.name: update
values.comment:
The date picker update function.  The third value in the returned
tuple represents the picked date, it is `Nothing` if no date was
picked or if the previously-picked date has not changed and `Just`
some date if it has.
values.type: Msg -> DatePicker -> ( DatePicker, Cmd Msg, Maybe Date )
